hygiene.tip: Wash your hands with soap and water for at least 20 seconds before eating and after using the toilet.
Drink clean, filtered or boiled water. Avoid drinking water from open sources.
Keep food covered and eat freshly cooked meals. Avoid eating stale food.
Cut your nails regularly and keep them clean to prevent infections.
Use a toilet and maintain cleanliness around your home. Open defecation causes disease spread.

seasonal_monsoon.tip: Prevent mosquito breeding by not allowing water to stagnate around your home.
Use mosquito nets or repellents to protect against dengue and malaria.
Avoid walking in stagnant or flood water to prevent leptospirosis.
Eat warm, freshly cooked meals during monsoon. Avoid street food and raw vegetables.

seasonal_summer.tip: Drink at least 8-10 glasses of water daily. Add ORS if feeling dehydrated.
Avoid going out in peak sun (12 PM - 3 PM). Wear a hat or use an umbrella.
Eat light meals with fruits like watermelon, cucumber, and buttermilk.

seasonal_winter.tip: Keep yourself warm and cover your head and ears in cold weather.
Eat warm foods and drink warm water. Include ginger and turmeric in your diet.
Wash hands frequently to prevent cold and flu transmission.

general.tip: Get at least 7-8 hours of sleep every night for good health.
Stay physically active. Walk for at least 30 minutes daily.
Do not ignore persistent symptoms like fever, cough, or weight loss. Visit a doctor.
Get all vaccinations on time, especially for children under 5 years.

disclaimer: These are general health tips for awareness only. Please consult a healthcare professional for personalized advice.
